date_provenance_analysis.schedule_date_source: FotMob API leagues?id=53&season=20252026 source inventory overview.matches.allMatches
date_provenance_analysis.schedule_date_authority: league schedule listing via FotMob public API
date_provenance_analysis.schedule_date_represents: scheduled match date as listed in the league season overview
date_provenance_analysis.detail_date_source: pageProps.general.matchTimeUTC from detail page fetched via schedule pageUrl
date_provenance_analysis.detail_date_authority: detail page canonical match, which may differ from schedule target when slug is reused
date_provenance_analysis.detail_date_represents: actual match date of the canonical/recent fixture served by FotMob for that pageUrl slug
date_provenance_analysis.date_source_divergence: schedule date comes from overview listing; detail date comes from detail page which may resolve to a different fixture when pageUrl slug is shared across fixtures

root_cause.explanation: FotMob uses the same pageUrl base slug for both home and away fixtures between the same two teams within a season. The slug /matches/{home}-vs-{away}/{slug} is shared. When the fetcher navigates to the schedule target's pageUrl, FotMob serves the most recent/canonical fixture for that slug, which is typically the reverse/second-leg fixture. The schedule external_id and detail external_id differ because they represent distinct fixtures that share the same pageUrl base.
supporting_evidence: All 8 pairs share the same pageUrl base
All 8 pairs have team names in reversed home/away order
Schedule dates are all Aug-Sep 2025 (early season, Matchdays 1-7)
Detail dates are all Jan-May 2026 (mid-to-late season)
Detail dates cluster in two groups: 5 on May 17 2026 (likely Matchday 38), 3 on Jan 17-24 2026 (mid-season)
Schedule external_ids and detail external_ids are distinct and non-overlapping
No multiple detail IDs per schedule ID or vice versa
Source inventory confirms schedule IDs are from league overview allMatches

reverse_fixture_second_leg.explanation: Schedule ID points to the first meeting; detail ID points to the reverse fixture. The team pair is reversed, consistent with home/away swap for the return fixture.

cross_season_slug_reuse.explanation: All mismatches are within the same 2025/2026 season. No cross-season slug reuse detected.

timezone_only_mismatch.explanation: Date gaps of 118-275 days cannot be explained by timezone differences alone.

cluster_1_matchday_38_likely.interpretation: Schedule targets from early-season Matchdays 1-7. Detail reverse fixtures all from the final matchday (Matchday 38) of Ligue 1 2025/2026.

cluster_2_mid_season_likely.interpretation: Schedule targets from early-season Matchdays 1-7. Detail reverse fixtures from mid-season (approximately Matchday 19-20) of Ligue 1 2025/2026.

pageurl_base_as_mapping_anchor_assessmen.rationale: A shared pageUrl base confirms the same team pair but does not distinguish between first-leg and second-leg fixtures. Date mismatch of 118-275 days indicates different matches. pageUrl base alone is insufficient for identity mapping acceptance.
requires_additional_evidence: exact date match or explicit date compatibility explanation
confirmed schedule external_id and detail external_id represent the same fixture
human review of the specific fixture identity

reverse_fixture_detected.condition: same pageUrl base, reversed teams, date gap > 30d
reverse_fixture_detected.action: The schedule and detail IDs represent different matches. The mapping is an identity mismatch, not an identity mapping. Do not accept.

postponed_or_rescheduled.condition: same teams, same pageUrl base, date gap < 30d, explicit reschedule evidence

unresolved_large_gap.action: Block until root cause is explained and classified.

proposed_date_resolution_statuses.date_match: schedule and detail dates match exactly
proposed_date_resolution_statuses.same_utc_day: dates are on the same UTC calendar day
proposed_date_resolution_statuses.timezone_only_mismatch: dates differ only by timezone offset, same UTC instant
proposed_date_resolution_statuses.within_tolerance: dates differ by a small, explained amount (e.g., rescheduled same week)
proposed_date_resolution_statuses.postponed_or_rescheduled_explained: date difference has a documented postponement/reschedule reason
proposed_date_resolution_statuses.reverse_fixture_detected: schedule and detail represent different fixtures (home/away reverse, same season)
proposed_date_resolution_statuses.cross_season_slug_reuse: pageUrl slug reused for a different season entirely

application_to_8_candidates.all_8_recommended_action: These are identity mismatches, not identity mappings. The schedule external_id and detail external_id should NOT be accepted as a mapping because they represent different fixtures. Instead, the fetcher should be fixed to navigate to the correct fixture (using fragment anchor or direct detail API).
application_to_8_candidates.mapping_acceptance_not_applicable: For reverse fixtures, the correct remediation is not identity mapping acceptance but rather fetcher route correction. The schedule ID should either fetch its own detail page (not the reverse fixture) or the pageUrl should include the specific fixture identifier.

recommended_remediation.short_term: Classify all 8 candidates as reverse_fixture_detected (identity mismatch, not mapping). Block all from acceptance.
recommended_remediation.medium_term: Implement date compatibility rule engine in fetcher/route normalizer to detect reverse fixtures before attempting identity mapping.
recommended_remediation.long_term: Fix fetcher route to use schedule external_id as fragment anchor (#4830466) when navigating to detail page, ensuring the correct fixture is loaded.
recommended_remediation.note: None of these remediations involve DB writes, identity mapping acceptance, baseline acceptance, or raw write retry.
